title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: zeroserve high-performance, frontend-agnostic transport layer R ecosystem. primary goal enable zero-copy binary transport massive datasets backends (DuckDB, Arrow, sf) modern web frontends serving local HTTP server.

title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: currently implementing Grand Rollout Plan (see roadmap.md context, though untracked). focus building minimal, robust transport layer without getting bogged mapping styling logic.

title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: starting Phase 1: Minimal Core Transport. - Objective: Create zs_serve_arrow() zs_serve_parquet() APIs. - Reference Code: Existing prototype logic moved R_drafts/. Use files source truth previously explored implementation details, aim clean, production-ready implementation R/ directory.

title: Agent Instructions: zeroserve
text: Minimalism: Stick transport layer. Don’t re-implement mapping logic belongs mapgl geoarrowDeckglLayers. Performance: Prioritize zero-copy binary paths (Arrow, ALTREP). TDD: Follow project’s TDD workflow defined conductor/workflow.md. Interoperability: Ensure generated URLs work seamlessly geoarrowWidget.

what: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: high-performance, frontend-agnostic transport layer streams large datasets directly browser use htmlwidgets, Shiny apps, custom web frontends. zeroserve leverages zero-copy Arrow IPC streams (via shared memory) HTTP Range requests (disk-based files) bypass bottlenecks JSON serialization. general-purpose, provides specialized support DuckDB, sf, Arrow objects, bridging R memory web visualizations minimal overhead.

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: can install development version zeroserve GitHub :

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: Serve sf object directly R memory local URL zero copies.
code: library(zeroserve)  # Load some spatial data nc <- sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE)  # Serve it! url <- zs_serve_arrow(nc) # [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8080/stream.arrow"

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: Serve massive DuckDB table Parquet stream supporting HTTP Range requests.
code: library(DBI) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb())  # ... perform massive DuckDB operations ...  # Serve the result as a streamable Parquet file url <- zs_serve_parquet(con, "SELECT * FROM massive_table") # [1] "http://127.0.0.1:8080/stream.parquet"

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving
text: Server: call zs_serve_* function, zeroserve spawns lightweight background R process (via callr) running httpuv server. Arrow, data shared via POSIX shared memory (using mori), allowing background server read R’s memory zero overhead. Parquet, data written temporary file, server hosts support partial content requests. Lifecycle: background server associated resources automatically cleaned R session ends package unloaded.

title: zeroserve: High-Performance Inter-Process Communication and Data Serving

text: Serve data via --Core Parquet export

title: Serve data via Out-Of-Core Parquet export — zs_serve_parquet
code: zs_serve_parquet(   data,   query = NULL,   engine = c("duckdb", "arrow"),   layer_id = "stream",   crs = NULL )

title: Serve data via Out-Of-Core Parquet export — zs_serve_parquet
text: data DuckDB connection spatial object (sf, data.frame, etc.). query SQL query string table name (required 'data' connection). engine engine use writing Parquet: "duckdb" "arrow". layer_id unique identifier data stream. crs Optional Coordinate Reference System.

title: Serve data via Out-Of-Core Parquet export — zs_serve_parquet
text: URL string pointing localhost background server.
